core_mechanism: a driven, damped second-order gradient dynamical system in which a state-dependent (coupling-induced) friction term balances a potential-gradient driving force, producing an extended near-constant-velocity attractor regime whose parameters shift observable output statistics
brief_reason: The abstract frames the analysis as a competition between a 'potential force' and a 'coupling-induced friction' that, when balanced for suitable coupling strength, yields an extended constant-roll regime governing the predicted observables.
